nodes.label: AI as coding assistant
nodes.label_negative: Manual analysis of lengthy or unfamiliar code
nodes.grounding_span: I will default to AI if the code block in question is very lengthy (more than 150 lines of code)

nodes.label: AI as knowledge gap filler
nodes.grounding_span: I will also default to AI if the domain of expertise is outside of my knowledge base

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I'll engage in a conversation about which technologies would fit that particular project

nodes.label: AI refinement and finessing of copy
nodes.grounding_span: I will not copy more than 200 lines of code from a LLM though, I like to know exactly what I'm copying over

nodes.label: AI as productivity enhancer
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to core skills
nodes.grounding_span: these tools make us more proficient

nodes.label: AI memory reliability in long conversations
nodes.label_negative: AI with sufficient context providing clear solutions
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes it's difficult to give the LLMs enough context to figure out precisely what the issue is

nodes.label: AI as future overseer of AI agents
nodes.label_negative: AI as direct doer without human oversight
nodes.grounding_span: I imagine I'll end up configuring AI agents at some point to do the brunt of my work, perhaps with me checking the output

nodes.label: Personal problem-solving enjoyment and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to solve things myself, as it keeps my mental problem solving skills sharp

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: these tools make us more proficient

nodes.label: Control and awareness over code
nodes.grounding_span: I like to know exactly what I'm copying over and what the result of that code will be

nodes.label: Preference for self-reliance when capable
nodes.grounding_span: I will always default to handling a task myself

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: I'll rely heavily on AI, even for more simple tasks because I'm not as familiar with the code syntax

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's inability to maintain context
nodes.grounding_span: I've had conversations that seem to go in circles with no clear solution

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI agent oversight
nodes.grounding_span: I imagine I'll end up configuring AI agents at some point to do the brunt of my work

nodes.label: Proactive anticipation and steering of AI defaults
nodes.grounding_span: I will always default to handling a task myself

nodes.label: Selective code copying with understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I will not copy more than 200 lines of code from a LLM though, I like to know exactly what I'm copying over
